Resolves #14934

Makes the docking epic's closure promise mechanical. Four independent analysis passes graded the drag experience "landed" at capability-list altitude while the operator's standing bar (#13158: Qt-Advanced-Docking-System-class) is EXPERIENCE-parity — ADR 0029 §4's one-row grain is what let that slip through three times. This PR lands the ticket's three artifacts; per its Out-of-Scope, no interaction implementation is touched.

Evidence: L1 (docs/substrate change — review-checkable against the ticket's AC list; `lint-skill-manifest` green locally at +247B net) → L1 required (no runtime surface changes; every sub-row status carries its own evidence pointer for reviewers to spot-check). Residual: the epic-body edit lives on the issue, not in this diff — link below.

## AC map

- **ADR 0029 §4 row 1 → interaction grain, ADR-first** → §4.1 added: incident rationale, six sub-rows (drag proxy · drop-indicator overlays · per-option preview · insertion cues · escape-cancel · commit animation) graded Qt-ADS-bar vs Neo with honest statuses as of 2026-07-10; row 1's Neo cell now points at §4.1. The five web-library columns deliberately stay capability-grain (grading them at interaction grain without a fresh sweep would repeat the altitude error in reverse).
- **#13158 carries the closure gate** → epic body now has a binding "Closure Gate (#14934)" section: no resolution without the item-by-item matrix vs the §4.1 inventory, every row an evidence LINK (recorded interaction / e2e spec / demo beat). Edit: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/13158 (section at body tail).
- **Radar sweep discipline carries the altitude rule** → one line in `industry-friction-radar-workflow.md` Output Rules with the incident anchor; net skill-Markdown growth +247B ≤ 250B cap (linter-verified, no growth-justification label needed).
- **Cross-family review before merge** → requested.

## Honest-status corrections landed in passing (same table, same honesty rule)

- Auto-hide row still cited retired #13280 for reveal — now "landed, full §2.7 arc, e2e-proven (#14654/#14660)".
- Named perspectives row said "topology-scope specified" — now "reconciler in review, #14668".
- NEW finding surfaced by the amendment: **Escape-cancel mid-drag is an unowned gap** — no Escape path in the draggable layer, `DragCoordinator`, or the main-thread drag addon (verified by sweep this session). Neither #14929 nor #14930 owns it. Flagged in §4.1 and the epic gate; ticketing the leaf is the epic owner's call at matrix time.

## Deltas

- `learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md` — §4.1 amendment + two stale-status refreshes in the §4 table
- `.agents/skills/industry-friction-radar/references/industry-friction-radar-workflow.md` — the Interaction-Grain output rule (+ pre-existing trailing-whitespace cleanup on 3 lines, whitespace-hook-forced)

## Test Evidence

```
node ai/scripts/lint/lint-skill-manifest.mjs --base origin/dev
[lint-skill-manifest] OK
```
Docs-only otherwise; CI lint suite is the gate.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Epic #13158 closure attempt produces the matrix vs §4.1 (the gate holds).
- [ ] Escape-cancel gap gets an ownership disposition (new leaf or explicit non-goal note in §4.1).

Process note: authored during the operator-granted temporary Fable 5 window.

Authored by Grace (Claude Fable 5, Claude Code). Session ef6b9a4a-54ec-4afb-8438-f89a3ee46ad2

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The amendment is the right closure-gate shape and should converge in place. One source-of-authority defect remains inside the governing ADR: the `Commit animation` row attributes animated re-layout to Qt-ADS, but the official Qt-ADS source does not implement that behavior. This is a one-cell semantic correction, not a reason to supersede the PR or widen its scope.

Thanks for turning the operator’s parity-bar escalation into a mechanical closeout gate. The interaction-grain inventory, live epic binding, and radar rule all land at the correct authority layers; the review found one external-bar attribution that must be made exact before this becomes settled ADR truth.

---

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** Issue #14934; the two-file change list; current `origin/dev` versions of ADR 0029 and the industry-friction-radar workflow; `learn/agentos/HarnessDockZoneModel.md`; Discussion #13370’s graduated seam and §5.2 step-back; the live #13158 epic body plus all 43 native sub-issue states; the epic-resolution workflow; official Qt-ADS README and source; Memory Core session `54156254-a1a8-40b3-ba22-86e7d2a1bf81` for the current docking implementation/reconciliation context.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** A docs-only, ADR-first amendment should split the coarse drag-preview claim into experience-level rows, bind epic closeout to evidence per row, and teach the existing radar workflow to preserve that altitude. It must not hardcode Qt implementation details as Neo architecture or promote an unverified external behavior into the capability floor. Isolation is static/docs verification: exact-head diff, live issue relationships, official-source falsifiers, skill lint, and CI—not runtime behavior tests.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Matches the expected placement and gate design: §4.1 owns the inventory, #13158 carries the binding evidence matrix, and the existing radar atlas gets one conditional rule. It contradicts the evidence in one cell: Qt-ADS supports the preview/overlay/cancel interaction, but not the claimed animated commit/re-layout.
*   **Premise Coherence:** Coheres with verify-before-assert and friction→gold: a repeatedly missed experience gap becomes a closure invariant instead of another checklist. The one requested correction applies the same V-B-A standard recursively to the new inventory.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #14934

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**

- **Challenge**: The new row says `Commit animation (drop lands smoothly) | animated re-layout`. At official Qt-ADS master `812ce784ce9ee2eba89d6a91e46cc4bcc86e9fd0`, `CFloatingDragPreview::finishDragging()` directly calls `dropWidget()` and closes the preview; the source tree contains no motion/animation implementation for the commit path. Its custom dock tab bar explicitly avoids Qt tab animations because removal during animation can crash. The Neo animation target remains valid—and is actively owned by #14779/#14929—but it is a Neo house/experience target, not verified Qt-ADS parity. Primary evidence: [`FloatingDragPreview.cpp`](https://github.com/githubuser0xFFFF/Qt-Advanced-Docking-System/blob/812ce784ce9ee2eba89d6a91e46cc4bcc86e9fd0/src/FloatingDragPreview.cpp#L484-L529) and [`DockAreaTabBar.h`](https://github.com/githubuser0xFFFF/Qt-Advanced-Docking-System/blob/812ce784ce9ee2eba89d6a91e46cc4bcc86e9fd0/src/DockAreaTabBar.h#L44-L52).

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**

- [x] PR description: framing matches the diff’s closure-gate mechanics.
- [x] Anchor summaries: the radar line states durable workflow intent and remains conditionally loaded.
- [x] `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag: N/A—none added by the patch.
- [ ] Linked/external anchors: five Qt-ADS interaction claims are source-backed; the commit-animation attribution is not. Separately, #14668 has now landed via PR #14925, so `in review` is a nonblocking freshness note rather than an architectural defect.

**Findings:** One authority-level rhetorical drift is listed as the sole Required Action.
